title: The Sky-Splash Mystery
cta: Shout RAIN out loud — I'm listening! And ask a grown-up to subscribe.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Oh! Oh oh oh! Whoosh — did you feel that?

scenes.visualDesc: Extreme close-up of Pip's surprised face, beak open mid-gasp, huge shining eyes, one wingtip touching the brim of her straw hat, the red barn soft behind her.
scenes.goal: Pip feels the first mystery drop
scenes.endPose: leaning in, beak open in a gasp
scenes.motionDesc: Camera pushes slowly in; Pip's eyes widen and she looks up, one wing rising toward her hat brim; a single drop plinks onto the straw.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Something went plip. From the SKY!

scenes.visualDesc: Medium shot pulling back: Pip stares up wide-eyed at one small grey cloud above the windmill, a dark wet dot on her straw hat, hay bales and the picket fence behind her.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: The sky never plips. Does it?

scenes.visualDesc: Wide shot of Pip pacing on the porch boards, curious head tilt, talking with open beak, the straw welcome mat and geranium pots around her.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: I will find that plip. Come on!

scenes.visualDesc: Low angle of determined Pip marching off the porch step, wings pumping, beak open mid-call, a tin watering can glinting beside the door.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Mo! The sky made a sound!

lines.speaker: mo
lines.text: Hmm. Show me where.

scenes.visualDesc: Medium two-shot among tall sunflowers: Pip gesturing skyward with an open beak, Mo thoughtful with a slow head tilt, blue butterflies drifting between them.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: It was a... a SKY-SPLASH!

scenes.visualDesc: Close-up of Pip inventing the word, eyes squeezed with delight, wings flung wide, while Mo watches calmly behind a sunflower stem, a ladybug on a leaf.

lines.speaker: mo
lines.text: The pebbles are dark and shiny.

scenes.visualDesc: Over-the-shoulder shot past Pip: Mo crouched low, pointing a wing at glistening wet pebbles on the winding path, moss tufts at the edges.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Wet! The path is wet!

scenes.visualDesc: Close-up of Pip's foot tapping a shiny pebble, her gasping face reflected upside-down in it, Mo's legs blurred at the frame edge.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Plop! There! Did you see?

scenes.visualDesc: Wide shot of the pond: Pip mid-hop pointing at spreading rings on the water, cattails swaying, Mo studying the surface from the wooden footbridge.

lines.speaker: mo
lines.text: Rings in the water. Watch.

scenes.visualDesc: Close-up on the pond surface, perfect circles widening past a lily pad, Mo's calm reflected face watching, a dragonfly skimming through frame.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: The leaves go pitter-patter.

scenes.visualDesc: Medium shot under the willow curtain: Pip listening with one wing cupped at her head, dappled light trembling on the mossy carpet around her and Mo.

lines.speaker: mo
lines.text: I will check the coop roof. Wait here.

scenes.visualDesc: Wide shot: Mo walking off through the hanging willow branches with a small wave, Pip watching him go, a white owl feather lying on the moss.
scenes.goal: Mo exits with a clear beat

lines.speaker: bo
lines.text: Pip! Why is your hat drippy?

scenes.visualDesc: Medium shot on the pebble path: Bo wanders in pushing her round glasses up, beak open mid-question, pointing at the damp brim of Pip's straw hat.

lines.speaker: bo
lines.text: A drip? A DRIP-TRIP?!

scenes.visualDesc: Close-up of Bo striking a dramatic pose mid-wordplay, glasses flashing, while Pip giggles behind a daisy, dandelion seeds floating past.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Look! The flowers are drinking!

scenes.visualDesc: Low angle through sunflower stems: Pip crouched beside a flower with a trembling water bead on its petal, Bo leaning in close behind her.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: The Big Oak is wet too.

scenes.visualDesc: Wide establishing shot of the Big Oak on its hill, bark glistening in patches, Pip and Bo tiny at its roots among scattered acorns.

lines.speaker: bo
lines.text: The swing has tiny silver beads!

scenes.visualDesc: Close-up of the wooden swing's braided rope strung with tiny water droplets, Bo's magnified amazed eyes behind her round glasses just beyond it.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: The whole hill is sparkly. Why?

scenes.visualDesc: Wide shot over the rolling hills, grass glittering with droplets, Pip and Bo small on the slope, the Big Oak silhouetted on a far hilltop.

lines.speaker: bo
lines.text: Maybe the clouds are crying happy tears?

scenes.visualDesc: Medium two-shot on the hillside: Bo flopped on her back theorizing at the sky, Pip frowning thoughtfully beside her, clover and dandelions around them.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Can YOU see the big grey cloud?

scenes.visualDesc: Medium shot of Pip turning to look straight at the viewer, smiling, wing pointing up past the oak branches, waiting warmly for an answer.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Yes! There it is. So big and soft.

scenes.visualDesc: Over-the-shoulder shot past Pip and Bo looking up: one plump grey cloud drifting over the oak's canopy, the knot-face on the trunk seeming to watch too.

lines.speaker: mo
lines.text: One... two... two-and-a-HALF...

scenes.visualDesc: Wide shot of the trio together on the hilltop under the heavy grey cloud, Mo counting down with one raised wing, Pip and Bo frozen in anticipation.
scenes.goal: Mo's stretched-count gag holds the moment

scenes.visualDesc: Pip freezes mid-step on the hilltop, eyes enormous, as soft silver rain begins to fall all around; she slowly lifts both wings and turns her face up to the sky, breath held, tiny droplets sparkling past her beak while Mo and Bo go perfectly still behind her.
scenes.goal: Silent visual beat: the rain arrives
scenes.endPose: wings lifted wide, face up to the falling rain
scenes.motionDesc: Camera pulls back slowly as rain drifts down; Pip lifts her wings and tips her face to the sky; Mo and Bo look up together; droplets catch the golden light.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: It's my sky-splash! It's RAIN!

scenes.visualDesc: Low angle of Pip shouting joyfully under the oak's dripping canopy, wings flung wide, Mo and Bo beaming beside her, rain sparkling like falling stars.

lines.speaker: bo
lines.text: Rain! Wheee! Rain rain rain!

scenes.visualDesc: Medium shot of Bo spinning in the rain with wings out, glasses speckled with droplets, Pip and Mo laughing, puddle rings spreading at their feet.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Dance with me! The rain tickles!

scenes.visualDesc: Wide postcard shot: all three chicks dancing under the Big Oak in golden rain, the swing swaying gently, the valley glowing warm behind them.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: So clouds hold water. Then it falls.

scenes.visualDesc: Medium shot of Pip explaining with proud little gestures beside a dripping sunflower, Mo nodding warmly, the rain easing to a last few drops.
scenes.goal: Pip restates the lesson in kid words

lines.speaker: mo
lines.text: It's a bit like when the watering can sprinkles.

scenes.visualDesc: Close-up of Mo gesturing gently toward the tin watering can by the fence, a calm knowing smile, raindrops still beaded on his red scarf.

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: What makes the rain fall down?

scenes.visualDesc: Medium shot on the porch: Pip looking right at the viewer with a warm expectant smile, Mo settled on the bench, lanterns starting to glow.
scenes.goal: Participation beat two: say the lesson

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: Yes! The clouds! You knew it!

scenes.visualDesc: Close-up of Pip clapping her wingtips in delight at the viewer's answer, Mo giving one slow approving blink behind her, fireflies blinking on.
scenes.goal: React as if the viewer answered

lines.speaker: pip
lines.text: What a wonderful day! See you tomorrow!

scenes.visualDesc: Slow pull-back of the trio waving by the barn at dusk, puddles mirroring the lantern light, the windmill turning gently as the world settles to sleep.
